jobText:
Strong Middle Frontend Developer
 Київ, за кордоном, віддалено
We are looking for a Strong Middle Frontend Developer to join the project full-time. This person will be the primary FE contributor — no splitting between projects. They need to be a thinking partner, not an executor — someone the client’s technical leadership wants to work with directly.



Your responsibilities will include:
Own any part of the frontend end-to-end — architecture decisions, performance work, integration, testing — without hand-holding.
Work directly with the client’s technical leadership as a thinking partner.
Navigate and extend a large, strictly-conventioned, module-based codebase.
Cover any part of the frontend independently — no “that’s not my area.”
Use AI-assisted development (Claude Code / Cursor / Copilot or equivalent) fluently and extensively as a primary tool.
What we expect from you:
Core stack:

TypeScript (strict) — no-any, no-implicit-any codebase; comfortable with generics, discriminated unions, unknown + narrowing, and yup.InferType-style inference.
React 19 + Next.js 15 (App Router) — understands the Server Component / Client Component boundary, server-side data fetching in RSCs, layouts vs. pages, route groups, middleware, Server Actions.
Modern React patterns — custom hooks as the primary unit of logic, controlled state, effects used sparingly and correctly, deliberate memoization; smart/dumb component separation.
State management — Zustand: store design, selectors, provider-scoped stores, SSR hydration concerns.
Forms — React Hook Form + Yup: schema-driven validation, server-error mapping back onto fields, controlled/uncontrolled trade-offs.
Styling — MUI v7 + Emotion: theme-token-driven styling, sx API, no hardcoded values.
Data layer — Axios: REST integration, interceptors, token refresh / 401 retry flows, typed API clients.
Testing — Vitest + React Testing Library: meaningful component and hook tests (queries by role/label, not implementation details).
Foundations:

Performance reasoning — can diagnose re-render storms, understand React’s reconciliation and memoization model, reason about bundle size, code-splitting, SSR/streaming, Core Web Vitals.
Architecture & data flow — can navigate and extend a large, strictly-conventioned, module-based codebase; respects existing architecture and knows when a pattern should be challenged.
Auth & app-level concerns — token-based auth, session handling, multi-tenant routing.
Browser & web fundamentals — solid on HTTP, caching, cookies, the event loop, rendering, accessibility basics.
Tooling:

AI-assisted development (Claude Code / Cursor / Copilot or equivalent) as a primary tool — non-negotiable.
Git discipline — conventional commits, granular atomic history, clean PRs.
ESLint / Prettier / strict CI — comfortable working inside an opinionated, heavily-linted codebase.
Nice to have:
Mapbox GL or other data-visualization / mapping / charting experience.
next-intl or other i18n frameworks.
Sentry or comparable observability / error-monitoring tooling.
WorkOS / Auth0 / Clerk or similar managed-auth experience.

responsibilities: Own any part of the frontend end-to-end — architecture decisions, performance work, integration, testing — without hand-holding
Work directly with the client's technical leadership as a thinking partner
Navigate and extend a large, strictly-conventioned, module-based codebase

soft_signals: Works directly with client's technical leadership
Full ownership — no splitting between projects
Respects existing architecture and knows when a pattern should be challenged
Comfortable in opinionated, heavily-linted, strictly-conventioned codebase
red_flags: Seniority title is 'Strong Middle' but responsibilities and autonomy expectations (architecture decisions, direct client leadership engagement, end-to-end ownership) are closer to Senior level
Single primary FE contributor — high bus-factor risk for the candidate
AI-assisted development listed as 'non-negotiable' primary tool — may not suit developers who prefer traditional workflows

tailored.headline: Front-End Developer | React, Next.js, TypeScript, Zustand, Vitest
tailored.summary: Front-End Developer with 5+ years of experience delivering complex features end-to-end in React, Next.js, and TypeScript. Comfortable owning any part of the frontend independently — from server-side data fetching and performance optimization to testing and REST integration. Experienced with Zustand, Vitest, React Testing Library, and AI-assisted development as a primary daily tool. Thrives in strictly-conventioned, module-based codebases and works directly with technical stakeholders to translate requirements into production-ready solutions.

bullets: Designed and implemented a monorepo architecture using Nx to unify three products — marketplace, admin dashboard, and landing pages — maximizing UI component and business logic reuse
Owned the frontend of a crypto marketplace (50K+ MAU), delivering token-based auth, a multi-step order creation workflow, and an advanced search and dynamic filtering infrastructure
Implemented WebSocket communication, enabling real-time chat messaging and transaction-status synchronization between buyers and sellers within active P2P sessions
Built a management and arbitration admin panel with financial dashboards, role-based access control (RBAC), subscription management, and dispute intervention tooling
Developed performance-critical marketing landing pages with Next.js, integrating product analytics, tracking scripts, and A/B testing configurations that boosted conversion rates by ~15%
Configured CI/CD pipelines, production monitoring, and code review workflows to maintain delivery quality across the full product ecosystem
Built a high-performance, SEO-optimized marketing landing page from scratch using Next.js with SSR, optimized page speed, and semantic HTML to support B2B user acquisition
Refactored the data-fetching layer using TanStack Query, adding caching, background revalidation, and consistent state synchronization across the application
Developed an isolated embeddable chat widget using Shadow DOM and WebSockets, enabling real-time AI responses on client websites with clean DOM containment
Implemented a subscription and billing system via Stripe integration, covering multi-tier pricing pages, billing management, and transaction status workflows
Improved Core Web Vitals (LCP, TTI) through code-splitting and lazy loading, reducing bundle size by ~10% and widget load times by ~15%
Integrated Sentry for error tracking and production observability, enabling faster debugging and issue triage
Built an Integrations Hub allowing users to connect third-party platforms (Slack, Telegram, Webhooks) to route leads and notifications from the chatbot
Contributed to building and launching the platform from scratch, delivering features across admin panel and marketing modules end-to-end
Built an SEO-optimized marketing landing page using Next.js with semantic HTML, custom meta tags, and responsive layouts to maximize search ranking
Developed data-heavy analytical dashboards with responsive tables (1000+ rows), pagination, transaction filtering, and interactive financial charts
Implemented OAuth integrations with Twitch, YouTube, and Kick for streamer authorization, profile synchronization, and live broadcast status tracking
Integrated REST APIs and WebSockets for global state management using Redux and real-time alert distribution

tailored.match_notes:
**Where you are strong:**
- React, Next.js, and TypeScript across 5+ years and three employers — solid foundation
- Vitest + React Testing Library explicitly in your stack — direct match
- Performance optimization is well-evidenced: bundle size, LCP/TTI, code-splitting, lazy loading, Core Web Vitals
- Sentry integration is a direct nice-to-have match
- AI-assisted development (Claude, Codex) is explicitly listed — this is a non-negotiable for the role and you have it
- Token-based auth, RBAC, and OAuth integrations demonstrate auth depth
- Zustand is in your stack and used in production

**Where the real gaps are:**
- **React Hook Form + Yup** — not mentioned anywhere in your master CV. This is a must-have. If you have used it, add it to your skills and be ready to discuss it in depth
- **Axios** — REST integration is evidenced but Axios is never named. If you have used it, add it explicitly
- **App Router specifics** — RSC, Server Actions, route groups, middleware are not evidenced. Next.js is present but the vacancy targets Next.js 15 App Router patterns specifically. Be prepared to speak to these in detail
- **Strict TypeScript patterns** — generics, discriminated unions, unknown narrowing are not evidenced in your CV. If you use these, surface them
- **MUI v7 + Emotion / sx API / theme tokens** — Material UI is listed but the vacancy wants theme-token-driven styling with the sx API. Clarify your depth here
- **i18n (next-intl)** — no evidence at all; this is a nice-to-have gap
- **WorkOS / Auth0 / Clerk** — no evidence; nice-to-have gap
- **ESLint / Prettier** — not named explicitly; add to skills if you use them (very likely you do)
- **Conventional commits / Git discipline** — not evidenced; mention if practiced

**Overall:** You are a credible candidate with strong production experience and the right core stack. The primary risk is the gap around React Hook Form/Yup and Next.js 15 App Router specifics — these are must-haves the vacancy tests for directly. Close those gaps in your skills list and be ready to demonstrate them technically.

level_checks.current_text: Front-End Developer | React, Next.js, TypeScript, Zustand, Vitest
level_checks.explanation: The title 'Front-End Developer' is generic and does not signal strong-middle seniority. It lacks any qualifier that communicates ownership, depth, or scope expected at this level.
level_checks.suggestion: Strong Middle Front-End Developer | React 19, Next.js 15 (App Router), TypeScript, Zustand, Vitest

level_checks.current_text: Designed and implemented a monorepo architecture using Nx to unify three products — marketplace, admin dashboard, and landing pages — maximizing UI component and business logic reuse
Configured CI/CD pipelines, production monitoring, and code review workflows to maintain delivery quality across the full product ecosystem
level_checks.explanation: 'Configured' is a passive, task-level verb that undersells the scope. At strong-middle level, this should convey ownership and deliberate engineering decisions, not just setup work.
level_checks.suggestion: Owned CI/CD pipeline design, production monitoring setup, and code-review conventions to enforce delivery quality and consistency across the full product ecosystem

level_checks.current_text: Built a high-performance, SEO-optimized marketing landing page from scratch using Next.js with SSR, optimized page speed, and semantic HTML to support B2B user acquisition
level_checks.explanation: 'Built' is a junior-level verb. The work described — SSR, performance optimization, SEO strategy — warrants stronger ownership language at strong-middle level.
level_checks.suggestion: Engineered a high-performance, SEO-optimized marketing landing page with Next.js SSR, semantic HTML, and page-speed tuning, directly supporting B2B user acquisition goals

level_checks.current_text: Implemented a subscription and billing system via Stripe integration, covering multi-tier pricing pages, billing management, and transaction status workflows
level_checks.explanation: 'Implemented' signals execution rather than ownership. A subscription/billing system with multi-tier pricing is a significant product surface that deserves stronger framing at strong-middle level.
level_checks.suggestion: Owned the subscription and billing system end-to-end via Stripe integration — designing multi-tier pricing pages, billing management flows, and transaction status workflows

level_checks.current_text: Contributed to building and launching the platform from scratch, delivering features across admin panel and marketing modules end-to-end
level_checks.explanation: 'Contributed to' is explicitly an intern/junior-level verb. Even if this was an earlier role, it actively undermines the strong-middle narrative and should be reframed to reflect actual ownership.
level_checks.suggestion: Delivered features end-to-end across admin panel and marketing modules, playing a core role in building and launching the platform from scratch

level_checks.current_text: Integrated REST APIs and WebSockets for global state management using Redux and real-time alert distribution
level_checks.explanation: 'Integrated' at this level is acceptable for a single integration, but combined with 'REST APIs and WebSockets' as a flat list it reads as task execution rather than design ownership. Redux global state architecture deserves stronger framing.
level_checks.suggestion: Designed the global state architecture with Redux, integrating REST APIs and WebSockets to power real-time alert distribution across the platform

level_checks.current_text: Owned the frontend of a crypto marketplace (50K+ MAU), delivering token-based auth, a multi-step order creation workflow, and an advanced search and dynamic filtering infrastructure
Improved Core Web Vitals (LCP, TTI) through code-splitting and lazy loading, reducing bundle size by ~10% and widget load times by ~15%

stack_checks.suggestion: Add a bullet to KYC Market or AMO FINTECH explicitly referencing strict TypeScript patterns: e.g., 'Enforced strict TypeScript across the codebase — generics for reusable data-fetching hooks, discriminated unions for order/transaction state machines, and unknown + narrowing for API response handling'
Version React explicitly in at least one KYC Market bullet; tie it to React 19 features (e.g., use() hook, Server Actions) to signal currency with the latest release

Version Next.js explicitly in the KYC Market role and reference App Router specifics: layouts, route groups, middleware, or Server Actions — e.g., 'Migrated routing to Next.js 15 App Router, leveraging route groups for multi-tenant layout isolation and middleware for auth-gated navigation'
Surface App Router usage in a KYC Market bullet with concrete patterns: Server Components for data fetching, layouts vs. pages separation, or route groups for tenant isolation

Add a bullet at KYC Market referencing Zustand store design specifics: e.g., 'Designed provider-scoped Zustand stores with typed selectors to isolate marketplace, admin, and landing-page state, handling SSR hydration to prevent client/server mismatch'

Add to a KYC Market bullet: e.g., 'Built the multi-step order creation workflow with React Hook Form and Yup schema validation, mapping server-side field errors back onto form fields for inline user feedback'
Pair with React Hook Form in the same KYC Market bullet; reference schema-driven validation and yup.InferType for end-to-end type safety between form schema and TypeScript types

Rename to 'MUI v7' in skills and add a bullet at KYC Market or AMO FINTECH: e.g., 'Styled all UI surfaces with MUI v7 using the sx API and design-token-driven theme — zero hardcoded color or spacing values across the component library'
Add Emotion to top_skills and reference it alongside MUI v7 in an experience bullet, noting its use for custom component styling within the MUI theme system

Add a bullet at KYC Market or AMO FINTECH: e.g., 'Built a typed Axios API client with request/response interceptors handling token refresh and automatic 401 retry, centralizing auth logic across all REST calls'

Reference ESLint and Prettier explicitly in the KYC Market CI/CD bullet: e.g., 'Owned CI/CD pipeline design enforcing ESLint and Prettier on every PR, maintaining zero-lint-error policy across the monorepo'
Pair with ESLint in the KYC Market CI/CD bullet as noted above

Strengthen the summary or add a bullet: e.g., 'Use Claude Code and Cursor as primary development tools — generating boilerplate, reviewing logic, and accelerating feature delivery across the full stack'
Add Cursor to top_skills alongside Claude Code and reference both in a summary sentence or dedicated bullet about AI-assisted development workflow
Add GitHub Copilot to top_skills if used; if Cursor/Claude Code are the primary tools, a brief mention in the AI tools section is sufficient to cover this requirement
If not used, omit — this is a nice-to-have. If interactive map work exists in the master CV, add a brief mention in the KYC Market or DonateSystem role
If no i18n experience exists in the master CV, acknowledge the gap in interview prep. If any localization work was done, add it to the KYC Market role with next-intl or equivalent named explicitly
Nice-to-have gap — no evidence in master CV. If any managed-auth provider (Auth0, Clerk, WorkOS) was used, name it explicitly in the relevant role's auth bullet
Nice-to-have gap — if Auth0 or a comparable managed-auth provider was used, surface it in the KYC Market auth bullet
Nice-to-have gap — same as Auth0/WorkOS; name any managed-auth provider used if applicable
